docs.text:
git -C /u/Git/CentralGauge commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
docs(site/p7): J — about#scoring, CONTRIBUTING, CHANGELOG, visual baseline

Mini-phase J of P7. Final docs + CI invariant updates.

- /about#scoring documents avg_score vs pass_at_n divergence (CC-3)
- CONTRIBUTING.md gains P7 lessons section
- CHANGELOG.md P7 entry
- Visual regression baseline regenerated for new leaderboard
  layout and new routes

Done-criteria check (Task J5) green.
EOF
)"

docs.title: Summary
docs.text:
P7 closes audit findings B-1 through C-4, I-1 through I-4, and the documentation findings (CC-3, CC-4 partial). CC-1 and CC-2 are acknowledged with operator/P8 deferral paths. The plan ships through 10 mini-phases, each one atomic commit:

  • A — Foundation extensions (acknowledge CC-1 per P6 runbook, CC-2 per P8 deferral; types; /api/v1/categories; /api/v1/summary)
  • B — Pass@1/Pass@2 visualization (multi-run SQL, AttemptStackedBar, AttemptBreakdownTile, SettingsBadge with consistency-guard, sort toggle)
  • C — Categories surface (/categories, /categories/[slug], leaderboard ?category=, /tasks column, filter rail)
  • D — Task Results Matrix (/matrix, /api/v1/matrix with task_set filter on all queries, TaskResultsMatrix widget)
  • E — Shortcomings UI shell (ShortcomingsSection w/ EmptyState, ShortcomingDetail without R2 fetch, wire to limitations endpoint)
  • F — Summary band + Performance vs Cost chart on home
  • G — Settings transparency on model detail
  • H — /changelog (markdown-driven) + banner callout
  • I — R2 transcript surfacing (incorrect-pattern dropped; only transcript link)
  • J — Documentation (about#scoring with multi-run rule + tasks_attempted distinction), CHANGELOG, visual baseline reconciliation, done-criteria

Total: 10 atomic commits, one per mini-phase. No commit produces an inconsistent working tree (Phase A's intentional svelte-check red is resolved by Phase B's first task — both phases ship in different commits but each commit's tests pass; svelte-check transition is documented in A-COMMIT).

Each task includes:

  • File paths (absolute when in commands; relative when in **Files:** headings)
  • TDD steps where applicable
  • Design rationales for non-trivial decisions
  • Verify steps before staging
  • Stage commands per-task; commit per-mini-phase
  • Per-phase visual regression baseline regen (B/C/D/E/F)

Phase G of P6 (custom-domain flip) remains held — P7 does NOT touch DNS or SITE_BASE_URL.

Operator vs plan responsibility split:

  • Operator action (gates UI population, NOT plan completion): run centralgauge sync-catalog --apply to populate tasks table (CC-1); ship the P8 shortcomings analyzer (CC-2). P7 ships UI shells that gracefully handle empty data; the CI invariants (Phase J) catch regressions early.
  • Plan responsibility (P7 ships): all UI/API surfaces with correct empty-state UX, correct multi-run aggregation semantics, consistent task_set filtering, back-compat preservation of existing fields.

Approximate work breakdown:

Phase Tasks New files Modified files New endpoints New routes A 7 ~13 (no diagnose scripts) ~3 2 (categories, summary) 0 B 5 ~7 ~5 0 0 C 5 ~6 ~4 0 2 (categories, categories/[slug]) D 4 ~5 ~2 1 (matrix) 1 (matrix) E 3 (E3 dropped) ~4 ~2 0 0 F 2 ~4 ~2 0 0 G 4 0 ~3 0 0 H 3 ~4 ~2 0 1 (changelog) I 1 (I1 dropped) 0 ~1 0 0 J 5 0 ~5 0 0 Total 39 ~43 ~29 3 4

Done-criteria (Task J5):

  • Leaderboard shows attempt-stacked-bars driven by tasks_attempted_distinct denominator; legacy tasks_attempted still emitted for back-compat.
  • Invariant tasks_passed_attempt_1 + tasks_passed_attempt_2_only ≤ tasks_attempted_distinct holds for every leaderboard row (jq assertion in J5).
  • Model detail shows breakdown tile + shortcomings section (with EmptyState when items=[]) + settings.
  • /categories renders cards; drill-down works; empty-state when CC-1 unresolved.
  • /matrix renders the dense grid filtered to current task_set on all SQL queries (CR-5).
  • /changelog renders entries.
  • Settings suffix appears next to display name only when consistent across runs; renders nothing when ambiguous.
  • /api/v1/categories + /api/v1/summary + /api/v1/matrix shape correct (data may be empty if CC-1 unresolved).
  • /about#scoring documents avg_score vs pass_at_n divergence, multi-run "best across runs" rule, and tasks_attempted_distinct vs legacy tasks_attempted distinction.
  • svelte-check 0 errors.

docs.title: P5.5 — Cutover (homepage move, indexability, sitemap, structured data, redirect, deploy) — Implementation Plan
docs.text:
For agentic workers: REQUIRED SUB-SKILL: Use superpowers:subagent-driven-development (recommended) or superpowers:executing-plans to implement this plan task-by-task. Steps use checkbox (- [ ]) syntax for tracking.

Goal: Land the public-launch atomic cutover. P5.5 is the last phase of the P5 series and ships a single deployable diff that flips CentralGauge from "private internal review" to "indexable public site". After this plan merges + deploys:

  1. Leaderboard becomes the homepage (/). The placeholder <h1>CentralGauge</h1><p>API-only build (P1). UI ships in P5.</p> at site/src/routes/+page.svelte is replaced by the leaderboard markup. The standalone /leaderboard URL is retired (with a 30-day 302 redirect; architect I4: temporary, not 301; see Mini-phase B).
  2. Indexability unlocks — <meta name="robots" content="noindex"> is removed from site/src/app.html, static/robots.txt is published with Allow: / + sitemap pointer, and the build emits sitemap.xml listing every public route (architect I9: artifact lives in .svelte-kit/cloudflare/sitemap.xml, not committed; served via the SvelteKit [assets] binding).
  3. SEO baseline — per-page <link rel="canonical"> populated by the layout, JSON-LD WebSite + Organization structured data emitted in <svelte:head>, OG tags audited.
  4. SSE route map updated — eventToRoutes() and the useEventSource(['/leaderboard']) callsites in 3 files swing to /. Existing SSE clients reconnect naturally on next page load (the worker reload during deploy evicts active streams; the client retry path opens fresh against the new path).
  5. All consumers re-pointed — Lighthouse URLs, Playwright specs, Nav active-route, internal <a href>s, doc references.
  6. Single atomic deploy — one wrangler deploy flips production. The branch is reviewed at canary (/_canary/<sha>/) before merge.

Architecture: Three structural moves (route file relocation, app.html meta deletion, layout <svelte:head> additions); one new static asset directory (site/static/) with a generator script (scripts/build-sitemap.ts); one new endpoint (/leaderboard/+server.ts 302 redirect, sunset 2026-05-30); existing-route patches (useEventSource(['/leaderboard']) → useEventSource(['/']) per architect I8: 8 occurrences in 3 files); 14 spec/doc updates. No new components, no new types, no new flags. The flag matrix carried forward from P5.4 is unchanged.

Design rationale: cutover is one atomic diff, not staged. Spec §11.3 P5.5 row says "Replace placeholder homepage with leaderboard; publish sitemap + robots." Single phase. We could split into "first publish sitemap+robots silently while still noindex'd, then a week later flip /leaderboard → /" but that doubles deploy risk for zero observable safety: external bookmarks to /leaderboard either redirect (if we ship the 30-day 302) or 404. There is no graceful "soft launch" mode we get for free by staging. Land it as one PR, review at canary, deploy once.

Design rationale: 302 redirect with a 30-day sunset for /leaderboard (architect I4). External bookmarks may exist (CI dashboards, Slack history, internal docs we haven't audited). A 302 (Found, temporary) redirect from /leaderboard → / is one cheap SvelteKit +server.ts per the Kit endpoints API. We preserve query strings (?tier=verified etc.) so bookmarked filtered views still land correctly. The redirect is set to expire on 2026-05-30 (30 days post-cutover) — at which point the route file deletes and /leaderboard returns the SvelteKit 404 (+error.svelte). The expiry is enforced by a CI test that fails 14 days BEFORE 2026-05-30 (architect C5 reminder-window pattern; not a runtime Date.now() > expiry check, which would silently flip behavior on an arbitrary day — bad). 302 is the right status code for a temporary redirect (the route is going away — we don't want browsers to cache the redirect indefinitely after the file is deleted). The sunset window is reversible: if we discover external bookmarks rely on the redirect, extend the expiry by editing SUNSET_ISO in the test; the redirect file itself is unchanged.

Design rationale: sitemap is static-at-build, not dynamic-at-request. Two options:

  • Static: scripts/build-sitemap.ts runs in npm run build and emits .svelte-kit/cloudflare/sitemap.xml from a hardcoded route list (architect I9: not committed). Deploys regenerate. Cacheable forever via the SvelteKit [assets] binding.
  • Dynamic: src/routes/sitemap.xml/+server.ts returns the list at request time; could include dynamic entries (e.g. one URL per indexed model slug from D1).

Static wins on simplicity, cacheability, and zero D1 cost-per-crawl. We don't currently have model/run/task pages we want indexed individually — they exist for direct link-share but the leaderboard is the canonical entry point. If P6+ wants per-model deep links indexed, swap to dynamic via a follow-up plan; the sitemap consumer (Googlebot) handles either format identically. Static-now leaves that door open without forcing the decision today.

Design rationale: structured data is layout-level (WebSite + Organization), not per-page. Spec §5.8 says "structured data via JSON-LD". We have two reasonable scopes:

  • Layout-only: one WebSite schema (with potentialAction sitelinks searchbox pointing at cmd-K) + one Organization schema. Both render on every page. Total: ~600 bytes JSON in <head>.
  • Per-page: layout-only PLUS each page declares its own Article/Dataset/SoftwareApplication/etc.

Per-page lets richer rich-results show in Google search (e.g. cards for /runs/<id> could link directly to the transcript). But it requires deciding the right schema per route (Article? Dataset? Report?), populating each loader with extra fields, and getting the JSON validated. Per-page is deferred to P6 along with structured-data validation in CI. P5.5 ships the layout-only minimum so we have something and search engines see consistent canonical + organization signals from day one.

Design rationale: SSE clients survive cutover via natural reconnect. Existing P5.4 clients hold open EventSource('/api/v1/events/live?routes=%2Fleaderboard') connections to the worker. At deploy time:

  1. Cloudflare gracefully drains the old worker version's open streams (typically ~30 s tolerance window).
  2. The new worker version receives traffic; its eventToRoutes() no longer maps any event to /leaderboard — it maps to /.
  3. The drained client's EventSource.onerror fires; useEventSource's 1s/3s/10s exponential backoff reconnects.
  4. But the client still sends routes=%2Fleaderboard because the page hasn't navigated. Server-side filter rejects every event (no event-routes overlap). The status pill shows "live" but no invalidations fire.
  5. The client only switches to routes=%2F when the user reloads or navigates.

This is the correct failure mode: a stale tab held open across cutover doesn't get garbage events, and the next interaction repairs the connection. The alternative — server-side aliasing /leaderboard to / in routePatternMatches — is more code and only papers over a transitional symptom that resolves on first page interaction. We document this in docs/site/operations.md post-cutover-checks instead.

Design rationale: bookmarked filter URLs are preserved through the 302. A user with https://centralgauge.sshadows.workers.dev/leaderboard?tier=verified&sort=avg_score:desc bookmarked must land on the equivalent / URL with the same filters. The +server.ts redirect captures event.url.search and concatenates Location: '/' + url.search. Tested in site/tests/api/leaderboard-redirect.test.ts — 4 cases (no params, single param, multiple params, encoded special chars).

Design rationale: cache invalidation is benign — old cg-leaderboard named-cache entries strand for ≤ 60 s. P5.1 introduced the cg-leaderboard Cache API named cache for the /api/v1/leaderboard?… API responses. The Cache API key is the request URL; the URL doesn't change at cutover (the API endpoint is unaffected, only the SSR'd HTML page route moves). However, SvelteKit's adapter-cloudflare also caches the SSR'd HTML response in caches.default keyed by the request URL — so old /leaderboard HTML may still be served by some colos for ≤ s-maxage seconds (60 s per +page.server.ts's setHeaders('cache-control')). This is acceptable: 60 s is below the time-to-meaningful-paint for most users, and the URL the cache was keyed on (/leaderboard) becomes a 302 redirect after cutover, so cache hits effectively serve the old leaderboard HTML while the URL bar redirects on the next navigation. We do NOT proactively purge caches.default at deploy time — Cloudflare offers no API to do so from the worker without a paid plan. Document this transient in operations.md.

Design rationale: prerender skip for / (architect R2). The prerender.entries config in svelte.config.js lists /about (the only prerender candidate today). The new / route is dynamic (per-request leaderboard data, SSE-tagged) and MUST NOT be prerendered. SvelteKit's default for routes not in prerender.entries is "do not prerender" — the new / inherits this default. To make the intent explicit and stop a future routes-config sweep from accidentally prerendering it, add export const prerender = false; at the top of the new +page.server.ts (covered in B2; see Task B2 Step 1).

Tech Stack: Same as P5.4 (Svelte 5.55+, Kit 2.58+, @cf-wasm/og, @noble/ed25519). No new runtime deps. One new build-time dep (deferred behind a build script — xmlbuilder2 is overkill for a hardcoded URL list; we hand-write the XML string). Sitemap generation lives at scripts/build-sitemap.ts and runs in the existing npm run build chain via a pre-build step. The static/ directory is created from scratch (none today per Glob('site/static/**') returning zero results).

Spec: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-27-p5-site-ui-design.md §5.7 (404 handling), §5.8 (SEO + structured data), §7.1 (Leaderboard at /), §11.3 P5.5 row (rollout sequence), §11.4 (pre-cutover gates), §11.6 (rollback), §11.7 (Domain + DNS — robots noindex removal), §13 (P5 done-criteria).

Migration map: docs/site/architecture.md lines 155-175 — authoritative surface list reproduced here for traceability:

Surface P5.4 value P5.5 cutover value Task that owns it Layout-server route /leaderboard / B1 / B2 Old leaderboard route +page.svelte + +page.server.ts deleted (Mini-phase F sunset) B3 / F2 Placeholder home +page.svelte exists replaced B1 <LiveStatus> SSE subscription useEventSource(['/leaderboard']) useEventSource(['/']) (architect I8: actual is 8 occurrences in 3 files — +page.svelte ×2 [migrated by B1], test fixtures ×5 [B5.2], doc-comment ×1 [B5.3]; do-worker.ts has zero occurrences); eventToRoutes() /leaderboard → / B4 / B5.1–B5.4 Lighthouse URL list 127.0.0.1:4173/leaderboard 127.0.0.1:4173/ D1 Nav active-route highlight pathname === '/leaderboard' pathname === '/' B6 Robots meta <meta name="robots" content="noindex"> present removed C1 Sitemap presence absent .svelte-kit/cloudflare/sitemap.xml emitted at build time, served via [assets] (architect I9: not committed) A1 / A2 robots.txt absent static/robots.txt published A3 Structured data absent layout-level WebSite + Organization JSON-LD C2 Canonical URL absent layout-level <link rel="canonical"> C3 /leaderboard 302 redirect (architect I4: temporary, not permanent) absent published, sunset 2026-05-30 B7 E2E specs ('/leaderboard' literals) 7 specs / ~16 occurrences / D2 useEventSource(['/leaderboard']) test fixtures 5 occurrences / (or kept as test cases for legacy route) D3 Doc references architecture/operations/contributing/design-system/changelog updated E1–E5

Prior plans:

  • docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-27-p5-1-foundation-leaderboard.md (P5.1 — completed)
  • docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-27-p5-2-detail-surfaces.md (P5.2 — completed)
  • docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-28-p5-3-cross-cuts.md (P5.3 — completed)
  • docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-29-p5-4-live-and-polish.md (P5.4 — completed)

Out of scope (deferred to P6 post-launch):

  • Per-page JSON-LD schemas (Article / Dataset / SoftwareApplication / etc.) — P6
  • Sitemap pings to Google Search Console / Bing Webmaster Tools — P6 operator runbook
  • Custom domain DNS (centralgauge.dev or similar) — P7 per spec §11.7
  • RUM regression alerting (Workers Analytics Engine + alarm) — P6
  • Marketing / launch-day announcement post — P6
  • Open Graph image audit per page (P5.4 shipped 4 OG endpoints; per-page coverage audit deferred) — P6
  • <link rel="alternate" hreflang> (single-language site today) — P6+ if i18n
  • robots.txt Disallow: /api/ (currently publicly readable; documented as intentional)

Wrangler [vars] SITE_BASE_URL provides the runtime value. site/scripts/build-sitemap.test.ts Unit test: snapshot the generated XML, assert all expected routes present, assert no broken-symbol output site/src/routes/+page.server.ts Replaces placeholder; lifted from src/routes/leaderboard/+page.server.ts verbatim except depends('app:leaderboard') (kept) and a renamed cache directive (unchanged in practice) site/src/lib/components/layout/StructuredData.svelte Layout-level <svelte:head> injector: emits WebSite + Organization JSON-LD; receives pageUrl (canonical) prop site/src/lib/components/layout/StructuredData.test.svelte.ts Component test: WebSite + Organization JSON-LD emit correctly; canonical URL constructs from $page.url site/src/routes/leaderboard/+server.ts 302 redirect from /leaderboard?<query> → /?<query> (architect I4: temporary) — preserves query string verbatim; sunset commented in-file 2026-05-30 site/tests/api/leaderboard-redirect.test.ts Worker-pool test: 4 redirect cases (no params, one param, multiple, special chars) site/tests/api/sitemap-served.test.ts Worker-pool test: GET /sitemap.xml → 200 + content-type application/xml + body contains https://centralgauge.sshadows.workers.dev/ site/tests/api/robots-served.test.ts Worker-pool test: GET /robots.txt → 200 + content-type text/plain + body contains Sitemap: site/tests/api/structured-data.test.ts Build smoke test: SSR'd / HTML contains <script type="application/ld+json"> with WebSite + Organization schemas site/tests/build/redirect-sunset.test.ts CI-time guard test: fails 14 days BEFORE sunset (2026-05-16) unless src/routes/leaderboard/+server.ts is deleted. Lives under tests/build/ because it imports node:fs — tests/api/** runs in workerd (no node:fs); tests/build/** runs via vitest.build.config.ts in plain Node (sibling test tests/build/rum-beacon.test.ts already imports node:fs). site/tests/build/sitemap-generator.test.ts The build-sitemap.test.ts referenced earlier — colocated with build-sitemap.ts under scripts/, run via vitest.build.config.ts. Snapshots the deterministic output of buildSitemap(); this is the single guard that the generator + the served XML stay in sync (no committed artifact to diff against — see I9). site/tests/e2e/cutover.spec.ts E2E that asserts the cutover invariants: / shows leaderboard, /leaderboard 302-redirects to /, robots meta absent, sitemap reachable

docs.title: Mini-phase A — Static assets + sitemap generator
docs.text: Lays the groundwork for indexability. New static/ directory with robots.txt + sitemap.xml, plus a build-time generator script. None of these change runtime behavior on its own — A1–A3 are pure additions. The cutover (B*) and noindex removal (C1) are what activate them.

docs.title: Task A-1: Create $lib/shared/site.ts — single source of truth for SITE_ROOT
docs.text:
Files:

  • Create: site/src/lib/shared/site.ts
  • Create: site/src/lib/shared/site.test.ts
  • Modify: site/wrangler.toml

The plan emits the absolute base URL (https://centralgauge.sshadows.workers.dev) in three places: sitemap, JSON-LD schemas, canonical link. Hardcoding it in three modules makes the P7 custom-domain swap a multi-file edit. Centralize once.

Design rationale: env var with default, not D1. The base URL is a config fact, not a data fact — it changes once per deploy environment, never per request. Reading from process.env at module-load time means: build-time scripts (build-sitemap.ts) pick it up via npm run build; runtime SSR picks it up from wrangler [vars]. Tests can override via vi.stubEnv('SITE_BASE_URL', '...') without touching code.

  • [ ] Step 1: TDD — write site/src/lib/shared/site.test.ts
import { afterEach, beforeEach, describe, expect, it, vi } from "vitest";

describe("$lib/shared/site", () => {
  afterEach(() => {
    vi.unstubAllEnvs();
    vi.resetModules();
  });

  it("defaults to the workers.dev URL when SITE_BASE_URL is unset", async () => {
    vi.stubEnv("SITE_BASE_URL", "");
    vi.resetModules();
    const { SITE_ROOT } = await import("./site");
    // empty string is treated as set; explicitly use undefined-mimic
    expect(typeof SITE_ROOT).toBe("string");
    expect(SITE_ROOT.startsWith("https://")).toBe(true);
  });

  it("respects SITE_BASE_URL when set (P7 custom domain swap)", async () => {
    vi.stubEnv("SITE_BASE_URL", "https://centralgauge.dev");
    vi.resetModules();
    const { SITE_ROOT } = await import("./site");
    expect(SITE_ROOT).toBe("https://centralgauge.dev");
  });

  it("emits no trailing slash (callsites append `/` for homepage)", async () => {
    vi.resetModules();
    const { SITE_ROOT } = await import("./site");
    expect(SITE_ROOT.endsWith("/")).toBe(false);
  });
});
  • [ ] Step 2: Implement site/src/lib/shared/site.ts
/**
 * Single source of truth for the site's absolute base URL. Read at module
 * load time from `SITE_BASE_URL`; defaults to the production workers.dev
 * URL.
 *
 * Set via `wrangler.toml` `[vars]` for runtime SSR, or via the build env
 * (`SITE_BASE_URL=... npm run build`) for build-time scripts.
 *
 * No trailing slash. Callsites that need `/` (homepage canonical, sitemap
 * homepage `<loc>`) append it explicitly so the canonical / sitemap /
 * JSON-LD all agree.
 *
 * P7 custom-domain cutover (`centralgauge.dev` etc.): change ONE
 * wrangler `[vars]` entry + redeploy. No code edits.
 */
export const SITE_ROOT: string =
  (typeof process !== "undefined" ? process.env.SITE_BASE_URL : undefined) ||
  "https://centralgauge.sshadows.workers.dev";
  • [ ] Step 3: Add [vars] SITE_BASE_URL to site/wrangler.toml

Insert one line into the existing [vars] block (anywhere — alphabetical preferred):

[vars]
LOG_LEVEL = "info"
SITE_BASE_URL = "https://centralgauge.sshadows.workers.dev"
FLAG_PRINT_STYLESHEET = "on"
# ... existing entries

cd /u/Git/CentralGauge/site && npx vitest run --config vitest.unit.config.ts src/lib/shared/site.test.ts 2>&1 | tail -10

Expected: 3 green.

git -C /u/Git/CentralGauge commit -m "feat(site): SITE_ROOT shared module + wrangler [vars] SITE_BASE_URL (P5.5 prep)"

docs.title: Task A1: Author scripts/build-sitemap.ts — emits .svelte-kit/cloudflare/sitemap.xml deterministically (architect I2 + I9)
docs.text:
Files:

  • Create: site/scripts/build-sitemap.ts
  • Create: site/scripts/build-sitemap.test.ts

Build-time script. Reads a hardcoded SITEMAP_ROUTES constant (sorted, deduplicated), emits a Sitemap-Protocol-0.9 XML document at .svelte-kit/cloudflare/sitemap.xml (architect I9: NOT committed; the SvelteKit [assets] binding serves it at /sitemap.xml). Idempotent — re-running produces byte-identical output. Wired into npm run build via a pre-step. Reads BASE_URL from the shared $lib/shared/site.ts module (architect I2 — single source of truth via SITE_BASE_URL env var).

  • [ ] Step 1: TDD — write site/scripts/build-sitemap.test.ts
import { describe, expect, it } from "vitest";
import { BASE_URL, buildSitemap, SITEMAP_ROUTES } from "./build-sitemap";

describe("buildSitemap", () => {
  it("emits valid XML 1.0 declaration", () => {
    const xml = buildSitemap();
    expect(xml.startsWith('<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>')).toBe(true);
  });

  it("uses sitemaps.org schema namespace", () => {
    const xml = buildSitemap();
    expect(xml).toContain(
      'xmlns="http://www.sitemaps.org/schemas/sitemap/0.9"',
    );
  });

  it("contains a <url><loc> entry for every SITEMAP_ROUTES path", () => {
    const xml = buildSitemap();
    for (const route of SITEMAP_ROUTES) {
      const expected = `<loc>${BASE_URL}${route === "/" ? "/" : route}</loc>`;
      expect(xml).toContain(expected);
    }
  });

  it("emits the homepage with explicit trailing slash to match canonical", () => {
    const xml = buildSitemap();
    // /  → https://centralgauge.sshadows.workers.dev/   (matches the
    // canonical `<link rel="canonical">` for `/`, see Task C3).
    expect(xml).toContain(`<loc>${BASE_URL}/</loc>`);
  });

  it("SITEMAP_ROUTES is sorted, deduplicated", () => {
    const sorted = [...SITEMAP_ROUTES].sort();
    expect(SITEMAP_ROUTES).toEqual(sorted);
    expect(new Set(SITEMAP_ROUTES).size).toBe(SITEMAP_ROUTES.length);
  });

  it("does NOT include /leaderboard (cutover sunset)", () => {
    expect(SITEMAP_ROUTES).not.toContain("/leaderboard");
  });

  it("does NOT include /api/ paths or _canary/", () => {
    for (const route of SITEMAP_ROUTES) {
      expect(route.startsWith("/api/")).toBe(false);
      expect(route.startsWith("/_canary/")).toBe(false);
    }
  });

  it("does NOT include OG image endpoints", () => {
    for (const route of SITEMAP_ROUTES) {
      expect(route.startsWith("/og/")).toBe(false);
    }
  });

  it("emits is_idempotent: build-then-build = byte-identical", () => {
    const a = buildSitemap();
    const b = buildSitemap();
    expect(a).toBe(b);
  });
});
  • [ ] Step 2: Verify failure
cd /u/Git/CentralGauge/site && npx vitest run --config vitest.unit.config.ts scripts/build-sitemap.test.ts 2>&1 | tail -10

Expected: FAIL — module not found.

  • [ ] Step 3: Implement site/scripts/build-sitemap.ts
#!/usr/bin/env tsx
/**
 * Sitemap generator. Run at build time (`npm run build` pre-step). Emits
 * `.svelte-kit/cloudflare/sitemap.xml` from a hardcoded route list.
 *
 * Why static, not dynamic at request time: simpler, cacheable forever,
 * zero D1 cost-per-crawl, deterministic (test snapshot bites). When P6+
 * wants per-model deep links indexed, swap to dynamic via a follow-up
 * plan; consumers (Googlebot) handle either format identically.
 *
 * Why hardcoded routes (not D1-driven): the public route list is a
 * design decision, not a data fact. We choose to advertise the
 * leaderboard, the eight cross-cut surfaces, and About — but not
 * `/runs/<id>` (those are deep links, not landing pages users would
 * enter via search). Hardcoding makes that explicit and PR-reviewable.
 *
 * Sunset of /leaderboard: NOT included. Pre-cutover the route was
 * accessible at /leaderboard but noindex'd, so it never made it into
 * any prior sitemap. Post-cutover it's a 302 redirect — listing it
 * would tell crawlers to follow the redirect (wasteful) or worse,
 * index it alongside / (duplicate-content signal).
 */
import { mkdirSync, writeFileSync } from "node:fs";
import { dirname, resolve } from "node:path";
import { fileURLToPath } from "node:url";

// SITE_BASE_URL is the single source of truth. Read at module-load time
// so build-time invocations (`npm run build`) and tests both pick up the
// same value. Default mirrors `wrangler.toml` `[vars] SITE_BASE_URL`.
// When P7 lands a custom domain (e.g. `centralgauge.dev`), set
// `SITE_BASE_URL=https://centralgauge.dev` in the build environment AND
// edit `wrangler.toml` — both paths re-target atomically.
export const BASE_URL = process.env.SITE_BASE_URL ??
  "https://centralgauge.sshadows.workers.dev";

// ALPHABETIZED + DEDUPLICATED. Tests assert sortedness.
export const SITEMAP_ROUTES: ReadonlyArray<string> = [
  "/",
  "/about",
  "/compare",
  "/families",
  "/limitations",
  "/models",
  "/runs",
  "/search",
  "/tasks",
];

export function buildSitemap(): string {
  const lines: string[] = [];
  lines.push('<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>');
  lines.push('<urlset xmlns="http://www.sitemaps.org/schemas/sitemap/0.9">');
  for (const route of SITEMAP_ROUTES) {
    // Homepage emits with explicit trailing slash to match the canonical
    // `<link rel="canonical">` value (which is `${BASE_URL}/` for `/`).
    // Without this both the sitemap and the canonical disagree on whether
    // the homepage URL has a trailing slash, which Google flags as a
    // duplicate-canonical signal.
    const loc = route === "/" ? `${BASE_URL}/` : `${BASE_URL}${route}`;
    lines.push("  <url>");
    lines.push(`    <loc>${loc}</loc>`);
    lines.push("    <changefreq>daily</changefreq>");
    lines.push("  </url>");
  }
  lines.push("</urlset>");
  // Trailing newline — POSIX text-file convention; some XML linters flag
  // its absence.
  lines.push("");
  return lines.join("\n");
}

// Standard Node ESM entrypoint detection. `import.meta.main` is a Deno-ism
// (undefined in Node + tsx); using it here would silently fall through and
// always treat this file as a library import.
const isMain = process.argv[1] !== undefined &&
  fileURLToPath(import.meta.url) === resolve(process.argv[1]);

if (isMain) {
  // SvelteKit's adapter-cloudflare emits the worker bundle to
  // `.svelte-kit/cloudflare/`; static files copied into that bundle are
  // served by the [assets] binding. We write the sitemap there directly so
  // the artifact is rebuilt every CI run and never committed (see I9).
  const target = resolve(process.cwd(), ".svelte-kit/cloudflare/sitemap.xml");
  mkdirSync(dirname(target), { recursive: true });
  const xml = buildSitemap();
  writeFileSync(target, xml, "utf8");
  console.log(`[sitemap] wrote ${SITEMAP_ROUTES.length} routes to ${target}`);
}
  • [ ] Step 4: Verify tests pass
cd /u/Git/CentralGauge/site && npx vitest run --config vitest.unit.config.ts scripts/build-sitemap.test.ts 2>&1 | tail -10

Expected: 9 green.

git -C /u/Git/CentralGauge commit -m "feat(site/sitemap): add scripts/build-sitemap.ts + npm run build pre-step"

docs.title: Task A2: Verify the build emits the sitemap into the adapter output (artifact NOT committed)
docs.text:
Files:

  • Modify: site/.gitignore (defensive — .svelte-kit/ is already ignored, but document the artifact)

Per I9: do NOT commit static/sitemap.xml. The build script writes directly into .svelte-kit/cloudflare/sitemap.xml, which the SvelteKit Cloudflare adapter's [assets] binding serves at /sitemap.xml. The generator's determinism is enforced by scripts/build-sitemap.test.ts snapshotting the in-memory output. There is NO CI diff step against a committed XML file.

Design rationale. The committed-artifact approach (Plan v1) requires:

  1. Operator runs the generator after editing SITEMAP_ROUTES.
  2. Operator commits the regenerated XML.
  3. CI runs the generator AGAIN and diffs against the committed XML.

Step 3 is a tautology — the deterministic generator's output IS the committed XML by construction; the diff only catches operator forgetfulness in Step 2. Cheaper: never commit the artifact, run the generator inside npm run build, and trust the existing build-sitemap.test.ts snapshot to prove determinism. CI failures land in the unit-test layer (where they belong) instead of a separate diff job.

  • [ ] Step 1: Verify the artifact appears post-build
cd /u/Git/CentralGauge/site && npm run build
ls -la .svelte-kit/cloudflare/sitemap.xml
head -25 .svelte-kit/cloudflare/sitemap.xml

Expected: file exists; 9 <url> entries; <loc>https://centralgauge.sshadows.workers.dev/</loc> (with trailing slash).

  • [ ] Step 2: Verify it is NOT staged by git
git -C /u/Git/CentralGauge status -- site/.svelte-kit/cloudflare/sitemap.xml 2>&1

Expected: not listed (the parent .svelte-kit/ is gitignored by SvelteKit's default .gitignore).

  • [ ] Step 3: Defensive .gitignore documentation

Append to site/.gitignore:

# P5.5 — sitemap is generated at build time into the SvelteKit Cloudflare
# adapter output; not committed. See docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-30-p5-5-cutover.md
.svelte-kit/cloudflare/sitemap.xml

(Belt-and-suspenders: if a future engineer mv's the artifact out of .svelte-kit/ into static/, this entry stops them from accidentally committing it.)

  • [ ] Step 4: Commit .gitignore if changed
git -C /u/Git/CentralGauge add site/.gitignore
git -C /u/Git/CentralGauge diff --cached site/.gitignore
git -C /u/Git/CentralGauge commit -m "chore(site): document gitignore for generated sitemap artifact"

docs.title: Task A2.5: REMOVED (CI diff guard obsolete — see Task A2 design rationale)
docs.text:
The original Plan v1 added a CI step running the generator + diffing against a committed static/sitemap.xml. Per the I9 architect finding, that is a tautology — the unit test (scripts/build-sitemap.test.ts) already enforces determinism by snapshotting buildSitemap()'s output. No CI guard required.

If a future change to SITEMAP_ROUTES lands without updating the snapshot, CI fails at the vitest run scripts/build-sitemap.test.ts step. That's the right place to fail.

docs.title: Task A3: Author static/robots.txt
docs.text:
Files:

  • Create: site/static/robots.txt
  • Create: site/tests/api/robots-served.test.ts

Single-purpose static file. Allows all user-agents, points to the sitemap. No Disallow: /api/ — the API is intentionally crawlable today (helps third-party data discovery; nothing sensitive lives there). Documented as intentional.

  • [ ] Step 1: TDD — write site/tests/api/robots-served.test.ts
import { SELF } from "cloudflare:test";
import { describe, expect, it } from "vitest";

describe("GET /robots.txt", () => {
  it("returns 200 with text/plain content-type", async () => {
    const res = await SELF.fetch("http://x/robots.txt");
    expect(res.status).toBe(200);
    expect(res.headers.get("content-type") ?? "").toContain("text/plain");
  });

  it("contains User-agent: * directive", async () => {
    const res = await SELF.fetch("http://x/robots.txt");
    const body = await res.text();
    expect(body).toContain("User-agent: *");
  });

  it("contains Allow: / directive (no global Disallow)", async () => {
    const res = await SELF.fetch("http://x/robots.txt");
    const body = await res.text();
    expect(body).toContain("Allow: /");
  });

  it("contains Sitemap: pointer to absolute URL", async () => {
    const res = await SELF.fetch("http://x/robots.txt");
    const body = await res.text();
    expect(body).toMatch(
      /Sitemap:\s+https:\/\/centralgauge\.sshadows\.workers\.dev\/sitemap\.xml/,
    );
  });

  it("does NOT contain a wildcard Disallow (would block all crawlers)", async () => {
    const res = await SELF.fetch("http://x/robots.txt");
    const body = await res.text();
    expect(body).not.toMatch(/^Disallow:\s+\/$/m);
  });
});

docs.title: Task A4: Worker-pool test for sitemap.xml served correctly
docs.text:
Files:

  • Create: site/tests/api/sitemap-served.test.ts

Verifies that the SvelteKit [assets] binding actually exposes the generated sitemap at the URL /sitemap.xml. (A1+A2 only verified the file is generated into .svelte-kit/cloudflare/sitemap.xml; this verifies it's reachable through the deployed worker.)

  • [ ] Step 1: Write site/tests/api/sitemap-served.test.ts
import { SELF } from "cloudflare:test";
import { describe, expect, it } from "vitest";

describe("GET /sitemap.xml", () => {
  it("returns 200 with application/xml content-type", async () => {
    const res = await SELF.fetch("http://x/sitemap.xml");
    expect(res.status).toBe(200);
    // Cloudflare may serve as `application/xml`, `text/xml`, or
    // `application/xml; charset=utf-8`. Accept any of the three.
    const ct = res.headers.get("content-type") ?? "";
    expect(/xml/i.test(ct)).toBe(true);
  });

  it("contains the canonical homepage <loc>", async () => {
    const res = await SELF.fetch("http://x/sitemap.xml");
    const body = await res.text();
    expect(body).toContain(
      "<loc>https://centralgauge.sshadows.workers.dev/</loc>",
    );
  });

  it("lists the 8 known cross-cut routes", async () => {
    const res = await SELF.fetch("http://x/sitemap.xml");
    const body = await res.text();
    for (
      const route of [
        "/about",
        "/compare",
        "/families",
        "/limitations",
        "/models",
        "/runs",
        "/search",
        "/tasks",
      ]
    ) {
      expect(body).toContain(
        `<loc>https://centralgauge.sshadows.workers.dev${route}</loc>`,
      );
    }
  });

  it("does NOT list /leaderboard", async () => {
    const res = await SELF.fetch("http://x/sitemap.xml");
    const body = await res.text();
    expect(body).not.toContain("/leaderboard");
  });

  it("declares sitemaps.org 0.9 schema", async () => {
    const res = await SELF.fetch("http://x/sitemap.xml");
    const body = await res.text();
    expect(body).toContain(
      'xmlns="http://www.sitemaps.org/schemas/sitemap/0.9"',
    );
  });
});
  • [ ] Step 2: Verify

Expected: 5 green.

docs.title: Mini-phase B — The atomic cutover (route move + redirect + consumer rewrites)
docs.text:
The structural core. Mini-phase B is the only block that, if reverted alone, undoes the cutover. Order matters: B1+B2 land together (replace placeholder + create root loader); B3 deletes the old route; B4 updates the SSE event-routing; B4.5 adds the SSE legacy-leaderboard alias for stale-tab support (architect I1); B5.* update the SSE consumer fixtures (architect I8: 3 files, not Plan v1's claimed 5); B6 fixes the Nav link; B7 publishes the 302 redirect.

Critical invariant (architect I7 fix): Mini-phase B must commit as a single git commit to master. Plan v1 split B into 6 separate commits (B1+B2, B3, B4, B5.x, B6, B7) which violated the "atomic unit" invariant — between commits, the working tree has inconsistent states (e.g., after B3 deletes the leaderboard route but before B7 lands the redirect, /leaderboard returns 404). Each B sub-task below describes the work; all changes are staged but NOT committed until the final Task B-COMMIT step at the end of Mini-phase B. Per-task git add is fine; per-task git commit is not. If reverting Mini-phase B is needed mid-execution, use git restore --staged --worktree <files>; if reverting after merge, git revert <single-sha>.

// Explicit: this route MUST NOT be prerendered (dynamic per-request data,

docs.title: Task B4.5: Add 14-day server-side SSE route alias /leaderboard → / (architect I1)
docs.text:
Files:

  • Modify: site/src/lib/server/sse-routes.ts
  • Modify: site/src/lib/server/sse-routes.test.ts

Architect I1 finding. Plan v1's design rationale claimed the SSE stale-tab transient was "transient until next nav". In practice, a tab held open across the cutover keeps routes=%2Fleaderboard in its EventSource URL until the user navigates — which could be minutes, hours, or days. During that window the tab gets ZERO events because eventToRoutes() no longer maps any event to /leaderboard. The status pill shows "live" but invalidations don't fire, leading to silently stale data.

Fix: add a server-side route alias in routePatternMatches() so an INCOMING subscription routes=%2Fleaderboard is treated as if it were routes=%2F. Outgoing event routes are NOT aliased (events are still tagged with the new / route). Sunset the alias when the redirect is deleted (2026-05-30).

  • [ ] Step 1: TDD — add tests to sse-routes.test.ts
describe("routePatternMatches — legacy /leaderboard subscription alias (sunset 2026-05-30)", () => {
  it("treats incoming `/leaderboard` subscription as if it were `/`", () => {
    // A stale tab pre-cutover holding `routes=%2Fleaderboard` connects after
    // worker reload; eventToRoutes() emits events tagged `/`; the matcher
    // must accept the legacy subscription pattern for the alias window.
    expect(routePatternMatches(["/leaderboard"], ["/"])).toBe(true);
  });

  it("does NOT match `/leaderboard` subscription against unrelated event routes", () => {
    expect(routePatternMatches(["/leaderboard"], ["/runs"])).toBe(false);
  });

  it("alias is unidirectional — `/` subscription does NOT match `/leaderboard` event tag", () => {
    // Sanity: `eventToRoutes()` no longer emits events tagged `/leaderboard`,
    // so this case is hypothetical; assert the alias doesn't accidentally
    // reverse direction.
    expect(routePatternMatches(["/"], ["/leaderboard"])).toBe(false);
  });
});
  • [ ] Step 2: Implement the alias in sse-routes.ts

Inside routePatternMatches(), before the existing pattern-matching logic, add:

const LEGACY_LEADERBOARD_ROUTES = new Set(["/leaderboard"]);
const LEGACY_LEADERBOARD_TARGET = "/";

// 14-day SSE alias for stale-tab support (sunset 2026-05-30 with the
// /leaderboard +server.ts redirect). Treat an incoming subscription
// pattern of `/leaderboard` as if it were `/` so a tab held across
// cutover still receives events.
const aliasedSubscriberPatterns = subscriberPatterns.map((p) =>
  LEGACY_LEADERBOARD_ROUTES.has(p) ? LEGACY_LEADERBOARD_TARGET : p
);
// Continue with existing matcher logic using aliasedSubscriberPatterns
// in place of subscriberPatterns.

(Adapt the variable names to match the actual function signature in sse-routes.ts.)

  • [ ] Step 3: Verify tests pass
cd /u/Git/CentralGauge/site && npx vitest run --config vitest.unit.config.ts src/lib/server/sse-routes.test.ts 2>&1 | tail -10

Expected: 13 green (10 existing + 3 alias).

  • [ ] Step 4: Document the sunset alongside the redirect

Add to site/src/routes/leaderboard/+server.ts doc-block:

// SUNSET 2026-05-30: when this file is deleted, ALSO remove the
// LEGACY_LEADERBOARD_ROUTES alias from src/lib/server/sse-routes.ts.
// The alias's only purpose is keeping stale tabs (with the legacy
// /leaderboard subscription) receiving events for the 30-day window.
  • [ ] Step 5: Stage (atomic Mini-phase B — no commit)
git -C /u/Git/CentralGauge add site/src/lib/server/sse-routes.ts site/src/lib/server/sse-routes.test.ts

Note: This task's edits to sse-routes.ts LAYER on top of B4's edits. Stage the same files; the patches are independent within a single working copy.

docs.title: Task B7: Publish 302 temporary redirect at /leaderboard (sunset 2026-05-30)
docs.text:
Files:

  • Create: site/src/routes/leaderboard/+server.ts
  • Create: site/tests/api/leaderboard-redirect.test.ts
  • Create: site/tests/build/redirect-sunset.test.ts

The /leaderboard URL gets a 302 (Found, temporary) redirect to /, preserving query string. SvelteKit's +server.ts GET handler returns a Response with 302 and Location header. Sunset 30 days post-cutover (2026-05-30) — the file is deleted then; the test guard from redirect-sunset.test.ts fails 14 days BEFORE sunset (2026-05-16) to force operator attention.

Why 302, not 301 (architect I4 finding): The redirect is explicitly TEMPORARY (30-day window). 301 ("permanent") tells crawlers to update their index AND tells browsers it's safe to cache forever; combined with Cache-Control: public, max-age=86400, immutable (Plan v1) the browser cache would still issue 301s long after the route file is deleted. 302 says "keep checking back" — exactly the semantics we want. We accept that crawlers MAY take longer to drop /leaderboard from their index; that's fine, since the URL still works (via the redirect) for the entire sunset window. Drop immutable from cache-control; cap max-age at 1h (3600s) so post-deletion-day clients re-fetch within an hour.

  • [ ] Step 1: TDD — write site/tests/api/leaderboard-redirect.test.ts
import { SELF } from "cloudflare:test";
import { describe, expect, it } from "vitest";

describe("GET /leaderboard — 30-day 302 sunset redirect", () => {
  it("redirects 302 to / when no query string", async () => {
    const res = await SELF.fetch("http://x/leaderboard", {
      redirect: "manual",
    });
    expect(res.status).toBe(302);
    expect(res.headers.get("location")).toBe("/");
  });

  it("preserves single query param", async () => {
    const res = await SELF.fetch("http://x/leaderboard?tier=verified", {
      redirect: "manual",
    });
    expect(res.status).toBe(302);
    expect(res.headers.get("location")).toBe("/?tier=verified");
  });

  it("preserves multiple query params", async () => {
    const res = await SELF.fetch(
      "http://x/leaderboard?tier=verified&sort=avg_score%3Adesc",
      { redirect: "manual" },
    );
    expect(res.status).toBe(302);
    expect(res.headers.get("location")).toBe(
      "/?tier=verified&sort=avg_score%3Adesc",
    );
  });

  it("preserves URL-encoded special chars", async () => {
    const res = await SELF.fetch("http://x/leaderboard?q=foo+bar+%2Bbaz", {
      redirect: "manual",
    });
    expect(res.status).toBe(302);
    expect(res.headers.get("location")).toBe("/?q=foo+bar+%2Bbaz");
  });

  it("emits cache-control: max-age=3600 (one-HOUR cache; NOT immutable)", async () => {
    const res = await SELF.fetch("http://x/leaderboard", {
      redirect: "manual",
    });
    const cc = res.headers.get("cache-control") ?? "";
    expect(cc).toMatch(/max-age=3600/);
    // Critical: NO `immutable` directive. The redirect is temporary; browsers
    // must revalidate so post-sunset (when the file is deleted) cached
    // clients learn the URL is gone within the hour.
    expect(cc).not.toContain("immutable");
  });
});
  • [ ] Step 2: Verify failure
cd /u/Git/CentralGauge/site && npm run build && npx vitest run tests/api/leaderboard-redirect.test.ts 2>&1 | tail -10

Expected: FAIL — /leaderboard returns 404.

  • [ ] Step 3: Implement site/src/routes/leaderboard/+server.ts
/**
 * 30-day sunset 302 redirect from the pre-cutover URL `/leaderboard`
 * to the post-cutover homepage `/`. Preserves query string verbatim
 * so bookmarked filtered views (e.g. `?tier=verified&sort=...`) land
 * on the equivalent filtered homepage.
 *
 * SUNSET: this file MUST be deleted by 2026-05-30. The
 * `tests/build/redirect-sunset.test.ts` guard fails CI 14 days BEFORE
 * sunset (2026-05-16) to force operator attention.
 *
 * Why 302 (architect I4 finding), not 301/307/308:
 * - 302 (Found, temporary): crawlers keep checking back; browsers cache
 *   only as long as `cache-control` allows. Right semantics for a 30-day
 *   sunset: post-deletion-day, cached clients re-fetch within max-age and
 *   discover the 404.
 * - 301 (permanent): crawlers update their index forever AND browsers may
 *   cache aggressively (combined with `Cache-Control: ...immutable`,
 *   indefinitely). Wrong for a route we're about to delete.
 * - 307/308: preserve method (POST stays POST). Not relevant here —
 *   `/leaderboard` had no POST handler.
 */
import type { RequestHandler } from "./$types";

export const GET: RequestHandler = ({ url }) => {
  // Preserve the query string verbatim. `url.search` is `''` for no
  // params or `?foo=bar` (with leading `?`) when params present.
  return new Response(null, {
    status: 302,
    headers: {
      location: `/${url.search}`,
      // 1-hour cache; NOT immutable. Post-deletion-day clients re-fetch
      // within the hour and learn the URL is gone. See architect I4.
      "cache-control": "public, max-age=3600",
    },
  });
};

  • [ ] Step 4: (merged into Step 3 — single response with cache-control)

  • [ ] Step 5: Verify

cd /u/Git/CentralGauge/site && npm run build && npx vitest run tests/api/leaderboard-redirect.test.ts 2>&1 | tail -10

Expected: 5 green.

  • [ ] Step 6: Write site/tests/build/redirect-sunset.test.ts

Architect C5 fix. The original Plan v1 test flipped behavior on Date.now() >= SUNSET_ISO. CI clock skew + the project principle "don't trust runtime date" make that fragile: the test could pass on day 1 and fail on day 31 without any code change, blocking unrelated PRs.

The reminder-window pattern below fails 14 days BEFORE sunset (2026-05-16). That window is intentional — it forces operator attention while there's still time to delete the redirect properly (open a PR, review, merge, deploy) instead of being woken up at midnight UTC on the sunset boundary. If the sunset window is intentionally extended, a single edit to SUNSET_ISO re-arms the guard.

Why tests/build/, not tests/api/. tests/api/** runs via vitest.config.ts using @cloudflare/vitest-pool-workers (workerd runtime; no node:fs). tests/build/** runs via vitest.build.config.ts in plain Node. Sibling test tests/build/rum-beacon.test.ts already imports node:fs — proving this is the right place.

import { existsSync } from "node:fs";
import { resolve } from "node:path";
import { describe, it } from "vitest";

const SUNSET_ISO = "2026-05-30T00:00:00Z";
const REMINDER_DAYS_BEFORE = 14;
// import.meta.dirname is available in Node 20.11+ (we're on Node 20+).
const REDIRECT_PATH = resolve(
  import.meta.dirname,
  "../../src/routes/leaderboard/+server.ts",
);

describe("redirect sunset reminder", () => {
  it("fails 14 days before sunset to ensure operator deletes the redirect", () => {
    const sunsetMs = new Date(SUNSET_ISO).getTime();
    const reminderMs = sunsetMs - REMINDER_DAYS_BEFORE * 24 * 60 * 60 * 1000;
    if (Date.now() >= reminderMs && existsSync(REDIRECT_PATH)) {
      throw new Error(
        `[CUTOVER SUNSET REMINDER] /leaderboard redirect must be deleted by ` +
          `${SUNSET_ISO} (≤ ${REMINDER_DAYS_BEFORE} days). Open a PR to delete ` +
          `site/src/routes/leaderboard/+server.ts AND remove this test, OR ` +
          `extend SUNSET_ISO if the redirect window is intentionally extended.`,
      );
    }
  });
});

docs.title: Task C2: Add JSON-LD structured data (WebSite + Organization)
docs.text:
Files:

  • Create: site/src/lib/components/layout/StructuredData.svelte
  • Create: site/src/lib/components/layout/StructuredData.test.svelte.ts
  • Modify: site/src/routes/+layout.svelte

Layout-level <svelte:head> injector. Emits two JSON-LD schemas: WebSite (with potentialAction.SearchAction pointing at the cmd-K palette URL — graceful degradation: external search agents land at /search?q=…) and Organization (CentralGauge metadata).

  • [ ] Step 1: TDD — write site/src/lib/components/layout/StructuredData.test.svelte.ts
import { describe, expect, it } from "vitest";
import { render } from "@testing-library/svelte";
import StructuredData from "./StructuredData.svelte";

describe("<StructuredData>", () => {
  it("emits WebSite + Organization JSON-LD scripts", () => {
    const { container } = render(StructuredData, {
      props: { pageUrl: "https://centralgauge.sshadows.workers.dev/" },
    });
    const scripts = container.querySelectorAll(
      'script[type="application/ld+json"]',
    );
    expect(scripts).toHaveLength(2);
  });

  it("WebSite script declares correct @type and url", () => {
    const { container } = render(StructuredData, {
      props: { pageUrl: "https://centralgauge.sshadows.workers.dev/" },
    });
    const scripts = Array.from(
      container.querySelectorAll('script[type="application/ld+json"]'),
    );
    const websiteJson = scripts.map((s) => JSON.parse(s.textContent ?? "{}"))
      .find((j) => j["@type"] === "WebSite");
    expect(websiteJson).toBeDefined();
    expect(websiteJson.url).toBe("https://centralgauge.sshadows.workers.dev");
    expect(websiteJson.name).toBe("CentralGauge");
  });

  it("WebSite includes potentialAction.SearchAction with /search?q={search_term_string}", () => {
    const { container } = render(StructuredData, {
      props: { pageUrl: "https://centralgauge.sshadows.workers.dev/" },
    });
    const scripts = Array.from(
      container.querySelectorAll('script[type="application/ld+json"]'),
    );
    const websiteJson = scripts.map((s) => JSON.parse(s.textContent ?? "{}"))
      .find((j) => j["@type"] === "WebSite");
    expect(websiteJson.potentialAction).toBeDefined();
    expect(websiteJson.potentialAction["@type"]).toBe("SearchAction");
    expect(websiteJson.potentialAction.target).toContain("/search?q=");
    expect(websiteJson.potentialAction["query-input"]).toContain(
      "search_term_string",
    );
  });

  it("Organization script declares CentralGauge metadata", () => {
    const { container } = render(StructuredData, {
      props: { pageUrl: "https://centralgauge.sshadows.workers.dev/" },
    });
    const scripts = Array.from(
      container.querySelectorAll('script[type="application/ld+json"]'),
    );
    const orgJson = scripts.map((s) => JSON.parse(s.textContent ?? "{}")).find((
      j,
    ) => j["@type"] === "Organization");
    expect(orgJson).toBeDefined();
    expect(orgJson.name).toBe("CentralGauge");
    expect(orgJson.url).toBe("https://centralgauge.sshadows.workers.dev");
  });

  it("canonical URL link is absent from this component (handled separately by Task C3)", () => {
    const { container } = render(StructuredData, {
      props: { pageUrl: "https://centralgauge.sshadows.workers.dev/" },
    });
    const links = container.querySelectorAll('link[rel="canonical"]');
    expect(links).toHaveLength(0);
  });
});

cd /u/Git/CentralGauge/site && npx vitest run --config vitest.unit.config.ts src/lib/components/layout/StructuredData.test.svelte.ts 2>&1 | tail -10

Expected: FAIL — module not found.

  • [ ] Step 3: Implement site/src/lib/components/layout/StructuredData.svelte
<script lang="ts">
  /**
   * Layout-level JSON-LD structured data injector. Renders WebSite +
   * Organization schemas in <svelte:head>. Mounted from +layout.svelte
   * so every page emits the same canonical metadata.
   *
   * Why two schemas, not one:
   *   - WebSite: Google sitelinks-search-box eligibility (cmd-K → /search?q=…)
   *   - Organization: brand-knowledge-panel signals (logo, name, URL)
   *
   * Why no per-page schema today: spec §5.8 says JSON-LD for SEO. Per-page
   * Article/Dataset/etc. schemas are richer but require deciding the right
   * type per route. Deferred to P6.
   *
   * pageUrl: the FULLY QUALIFIED canonical URL of the current page.
   * Computed in +layout.svelte from $page.url.href. Passed as a prop so
   * the test can inject deterministic values.
   */
  import { SITE_ROOT } from '$lib/shared/site';

  let { pageUrl }: { pageUrl: string } = $props();

  // JSON-LD XSS hardening helper. Even though today's schemas are static,
  // emitting `</` literally inside a <script> body can break out of the
  // tag. Standard Next.js / Remix idiom is to JSON.stringify then replace
  // `<` with its Unicode escape — the JSON parser still accepts the
  // escape, but no `</script>` sequence can appear in the output. Future
  // schemas with dynamic data (run notes, model descriptions) inherit the
  // safeguard for free.
  function jsonLd(schema: unknown): string {
    const safeJson = JSON.stringify(schema).replace(/</g, '\\u003c');
    return `<script type="application/ld+json">${safeJson}</script>`;
  }

  // Build WebSite schema. The `url` property is the SITE root (NOT the
  // current page) per https://schema.org/WebSite — the WebSite schema
  // describes the whole site; per-page canonical is the <link>.

  const websiteSchema = {
    '@context': 'https://schema.org',
    '@type': 'WebSite',
    name: 'CentralGauge',
    description: 'Open-source benchmark for evaluating LLMs on AL code generation, debugging, and refactoring for Microsoft Dynamics 365 Business Central.',
    url: SITE_ROOT,
    potentialAction: {
      '@type': 'SearchAction',
      target: {
        '@type': 'EntryPoint',
        urlTemplate: `${SITE_ROOT}/search?q={search_term_string}`,
      },
      'query-input': 'required name=search_term_string',
    },
  };

  const organizationSchema = {
    '@context': 'https://schema.org',
    '@type': 'Organization',
    name: 'CentralGauge',
    url: SITE_ROOT,
    sameAs: [
      'https://github.com/SShadowS/CentralGauge',
    ],
  };
</script>

{@html jsonLd(websiteSchema)}
{@html jsonLd(organizationSchema)}

Why {@html}: Svelte 5 renders <script> tags as text by default (XSS protection). Structured data is content-safe (we control the JSON), so {@html} is correct here. The jsonLd() helper escapes < to < so the output never contains a </script> sequence — defends against future schemas that ingest user-submitted content (model descriptions, run notes) without requiring per-call review. Pattern lifted from Next.js's <Script> and Remix's json-ld helper.

docs.title: Task C3: Add <link rel="canonical"> per page in layout
docs.text:
Files:

  • Modify: site/src/routes/+layout.svelte

A single <link rel="canonical"> in the layout <svelte:head> populated from $page.url.href covers every route. Per Google: canonical should match the URL the page would be shared as. With query params, the canonical typically excludes them (e.g. /leaderboard?tier=verified canonicalizes to /leaderboard) — but this is a hint, not an absolute. We choose to include the full URL (with query) for filtered views since they ARE distinct content from the unfiltered page (different table rows, different sort).

Trade-off considered: include vs strip query params from canonical.

  • Include: every URL is its own canonical. Filtered views get indexed separately. Risk: explosion of indexed URLs (the leaderboard's filter combinatorics × ~9 surfaces × ~50 query combos = ~450 URLs).
  • Strip: filtered views canonicalize to the unfiltered base. Search engines index one URL per surface. Risk: filter-bookmarkers searching for their filter find the unfiltered page, then have to reapply.

Pick strip for P5.5: simpler, fewer indexed URLs, cleaner sitemap relationship. Filter URLs are direct-share linkable but not search-discoverable. If a P6+ design wants filtered views findable, switch to include via a +layout.server.ts data field that the canonical reads.

  • [ ] Step 1: Edit +layout.svelte — ADDITIVE patch (do NOT overwrite C2's edits)

This task INSERTS one $derived line into the <script> block and one <link rel="canonical"> line into the <svelte:head> block. C2 already added the import { page }, import { SITE_ROOT }, and <StructuredData> mount; do NOT re-add them.

Diff context — <script> block. Find the C2-added imports + let { data, children } = $props(); line. Insert ONE new $derived declaration immediately after $props():

  let { data, children } = $props();                          // existing (post-C2)

+ // Canonical = SITE_ROOT + pathname (NO query string). See design rationale.
+ const canonicalHref = $derived(`${SITE_ROOT}${page.url.pathname}`);

  let paletteEverOpened = $state(false);                      // existing

Diff context — <svelte:head> block. Insert ONE <link> line immediately AFTER the C2-added <StructuredData> mount and IMMEDIATELY BEFORE the existing RUM beacon block:

 <svelte:head>
   <StructuredData pageUrl={canonicalHref} />          ← C2 (note: prop changed to canonicalHref)
+  <link rel="canonical" href={canonicalHref} />

   {#if data.flags?.rum_beacon && data.cfWebAnalyticsToken}

NOTE: Update the pageUrl prop on <StructuredData> from page.url.href (C2 default) to canonicalHref so the WebSite/Organization JSON-LD's url matches the canonical URL exactly. C2 used page.url.href only because canonicalHref didn't exist yet; tighten now.

  • [ ] Step 2: Build smoke
cd /u/Git/CentralGauge/site && npm run build && npm run preview &
sleep 3
curl -s http://127.0.0.1:4173/ | grep -i "canonical"
curl -s "http://127.0.0.1:4173/?tier=verified" | grep -i "canonical"
kill %1

Expected for both: <link rel="canonical" href="https://centralgauge.sshadows.workers.dev/" /> (query string stripped from /?tier=verified).

  • [ ] Step 3: Add a worker test

site/tests/api/canonical-link.test.ts:

import { SELF } from "cloudflare:test";
import { describe, expect, it } from "vitest";

describe("canonical URL link", () => {
  it("emits canonical for / pointing at site root", async () => {
    const res = await SELF.fetch("http://x/");
    const body = await res.text();
    expect(body).toMatch(
      /<link rel="canonical" href="https:\/\/centralgauge\.sshadows\.workers\.dev\/" \/>/,
    );
  });

  it("strips query string from canonical", async () => {
    const res = await SELF.fetch("http://x/?tier=verified");
    const body = await res.text();
    expect(body).toMatch(
      /<link rel="canonical" href="https:\/\/centralgauge\.sshadows\.workers\.dev\/" \/>/,
    );
    expect(body).not.toContain(
      'canonical" href="https://centralgauge.sshadows.workers.dev/?tier=verified"',
    );
  });

  it("emits canonical for /models", async () => {
    const res = await SELF.fetch("http://x/models");
    const body = await res.text();
    expect(body).toMatch(
      /<link rel="canonical" href="https:\/\/centralgauge\.sshadows\.workers\.dev\/models" \/>/,
    );
  });
});

docs.title: Task D2.3: Edit tests/e2e/visual-regression.spec.ts + REGENERATE baselines
docs.text:
The spec currently has { name: 'leaderboard', url: '/leaderboard' } at line 4. Change to { name: 'home', url: '/' }.

Architect C3 + C4 fix. Plan v1 directed git mv leaderboard-*.png home-*.png claiming the DOM is bit-identical pre/post cutover. Two reasons that fails:

  1. No baselines exist to rename. Verified by listing site/tests/e2e/__screenshots__/: only .gitkeep is committed; zero PNGs. There is no leaderboard-*.png to git mv.
  2. Even if they existed, the DOM is no longer bit-identical. The same Mini-phase B + C edits add (a) <link rel="canonical"> (C3), (b) JSON-LD <script> tags (C2), (c) potentially a different useId() sequence in the now-shared layout. Pixel-perfect rendering can shift via reflow or font-metrics differences.

Correct approach: REGENERATE baselines as part of the cutover, then commit the new PNGs alongside the rest of D2.

docs.title: Task D3: Author the cutover smoke spec tests/e2e/cutover.spec.ts
docs.text:
Files:

  • Create: site/tests/e2e/cutover.spec.ts

A single spec that asserts the cutover invariants end-to-end. Independent of every other spec — if this one passes, P5.5 is meaningfully done.

  • [ ] Step 1: Write the spec
import { expect, test } from "@playwright/test";

test.describe("P5.5 cutover", () => {
  test("/ shows leaderboard", async ({ page }) => {
    await page.goto("/");
    await expect(page.getByRole("heading", { level: 1, name: /Leaderboard/i }))
      .toBeVisible();
    await expect(page.getByRole("table")).toBeVisible();
  });

  test("/leaderboard 302 redirects to /", async ({ page }) => {
    const response = await page.goto("/leaderboard");
    // Playwright follows redirects by default; final URL is `/`. Use a
    // separate request to assert the initial response status is 302
    // (not the post-redirect 200 that page.goto returns).
    const initial = await page.context().request.fetch("/leaderboard", {
      maxRedirects: 0,
    });
    expect(initial.status()).toBe(302);
    await expect(page).toHaveURL("/");
  });

  test("/leaderboard?tier=verified preserves query through 302", async ({ page }) => {
    await page.goto("/leaderboard?tier=verified");
    await expect(page).toHaveURL(/^\/?\?tier=verified/);
  });

  test("robots noindex meta is absent", async ({ page }) => {
    await page.goto("/");
    const robotsMeta = await page.locator(
      'meta[name="robots"][content="noindex"]',
    ).count();
    expect(robotsMeta).toBe(0);
  });

  test("canonical link is emitted", async ({ page }) => {
    await page.goto("/");
    const canonical = page.locator('link[rel="canonical"]');
    await expect(canonical).toHaveAttribute(
      "href",
      /https:\/\/centralgauge\.sshadows\.workers\.dev\/?$/,
    );
  });

  test("JSON-LD WebSite + Organization scripts present", async ({ page }) => {
    await page.goto("/");
    const scripts = page.locator('script[type="application/ld+json"]');
    await expect(scripts).toHaveCount(2);
  });

  test("sitemap.xml is reachable + lists / not /leaderboard", async ({ request }) => {
    const res = await request.get("/sitemap.xml");
    expect(res.status()).toBe(200);
    const body = await res.text();
    expect(body).toContain(
      "<loc>https://centralgauge.sshadows.workers.dev/</loc>",
    );
    expect(body).not.toContain("/leaderboard");
  });

  test("robots.txt is reachable + allows all", async ({ request }) => {
    const res = await request.get("/robots.txt");
    expect(res.status()).toBe(200);
    const body = await res.text();
    expect(body).toContain("User-agent: *");
    expect(body).toContain("Allow: /");
    expect(body).toMatch(/Sitemap:.+sitemap\.xml/);
  });

  test('Nav "Leaderboard" link points at /', async ({ page }) => {
    await page.goto("/");
    const navLink = page.locator("nav a", { hasText: /^Leaderboard$/ });
    await expect(navLink).toHaveAttribute("href", "/");
  });
});

docs.title: Task E2: Update docs/site/operations.md
docs.text:
Files:

  • Modify: docs/site/operations.md

Per grep: 5 lines reference /leaderboard. Lines 61, 111, 175, 230, 237. Action:

  • Line 61 — /_canary/<sha>/leaderboard review checklist: REMOVE this entry (architect C6 — reviewing redirected URLs via canary leaks the reviewer out of canary scope). Replace with /_canary/<sha>/ direct review.
  • Line 111 — top-5 routes (/leaderboard, /models, /runs, /about, /compare): update to (/, /models, /runs, /about, /compare).
  • Line 175 — "Unauthorized leaderboard mutation" — conceptual, unchanged.
  • Line 230 — "/leaderboard cloudflareinsights" curl probe — update to /.

  • Line 237 — "Rename /leaderboard → /, retire placeholder +page.svelte" P5.5 row in the deferred table — mark DONE.

  • [ ] Step 1: Edit each line

  • [ ] Step 2: Add a "Post-cutover verification" subsection

### Post-cutover verification (2026-04-30)

After `wrangler deploy` lands the cutover:

1. `curl -sI https://centralgauge.sshadows.workers.dev/` → 200
2. `curl -sI https://centralgauge.sshadows.workers.dev/leaderboard` → 302; Location: /
3. `curl https://centralgauge.sshadows.workers.dev/sitemap.xml | head -10` → XML with 9 routes including <loc>https://centralgauge.sshadows.workers.dev/</loc>
4. `curl https://centralgauge.sshadows.workers.dev/robots.txt` → User-agent: * \n Allow: / \n Sitemap: ...
5. `curl https://centralgauge.sshadows.workers.dev/ | grep -c 'application/ld+json'` → 2
6. `curl https://centralgauge.sshadows.workers.dev/ | grep -i robots` → no `noindex` (only beacon-related directives if any)
7. `curl https://centralgauge.sshadows.workers.dev/ | grep canonical` → `<link rel="canonical" href="https://centralgauge.sshadows.workers.dev/" />`

### SSE stale-tab transient

Tabs held open across the cutover deploy keep the OLD `routes=%2Fleaderboard` query in their EventSource URL. After the worker reload they reconnect (3-attempt backoff in `useEventSource`), but the new `eventToRoutes()` doesn't map any event to `/leaderboard`. Result: the stale tab's `<LiveStatus>` shows "live" but no invalidations fire. The user's first navigation (e.g. clicking a sort header) triggers a fresh page load that opens a new EventSource with `routes=%2F`. No action needed — documented for completeness.

### Cache invalidation transient (architect R4)

The `caches.default` adapter cache may serve old `/leaderboard` HTML for ≤ 60 s post-deploy from some colos (s-maxage from the `+page.server.ts` setHeaders). After expiry, the URL hits the 302 redirect handler. Likewise, any pre-cutover `/` (placeholder) responses cached in `caches.default` may persist ≤ 60 s — colo cache hits serve the old placeholder HTML while the worker has already swung to the leaderboard. Acceptable: a 60-second transient is the smallest possible footprint of the cutover. Document user-visible weirdness in `docs/postmortems/` if reported.

> **Optional first-deploy mitigation (out of scope, deferred to post-cutover review):** the new `/` route's first deploy could ship `Cache-Control: no-store` to bypass `caches.default` until the cutover settles, then revert to the normal s-maxage in a follow-up. Not needed unless RUM shows a measurable spike in stale-cache reports.

### Cloudflare worker swap window (architect R1)

Cloudflare's worker version-swap is atomic per request but not instantaneous globally — the propagation window across data centers is ~30-60 s. During that window, some requests hit the new worker (302 on `/leaderboard`, leaderboard on `/`) and some hit the old (placeholder on `/`, leaderboard on `/leaderboard`). Internal links (Nav, anchor tags) work either way (the old worker's Nav still points at `/leaderboard`, the new at `/`); SSE clients on the old worker receive `/leaderboard`-tagged events and the I1 alias on the new worker accepts the legacy subscription. No user-visible breakage during the window; document for completeness.

### Durable-Object recent-buffer staleness (architect R3)

`LeaderboardBroadcaster.recent[]` retains pre-cutover `BroadcastEvent` records that are tagged `routes: ['/leaderboard']`. Post-cutover, when a fresh `EventSource('/...?routes=%2F')` connects, the DO's replay logic emits these legacy-tagged events. `routePatternMatches()` with the I1 alias accepts an incoming `/` against an outgoing `/leaderboard` event ROUTE? Verify in test — the alias is intentionally unidirectional (incoming `/leaderboard` → `/`, NOT outgoing). Result: legacy-tagged events DON'T replay to new subscribers. Acceptable given the 14-day SSE alias from I1 and the buffer's natural decay (size cap or TTL).

### RUM week-1 baseline (architect R5)

Cloudflare Web Analytics history per-route on `/leaderboard` is preserved (the route still serves a 302 for 30 days; the data point is recorded). Post-cutover, RUM data on `/` replaces what was previously captured for the placeholder. Week-1 post-deploy establishes a NEW baseline for `/`; week-over-week comparison against pre-cutover `/leaderboard` is apples-to-oranges. Document.

### Canary noindex preserved (architect R6)

Verify `_canary/[sha]/[...path]/+page.svelte` retains `<meta robots noindex>` so canary pages never get indexed even after the global noindex removal. The canary route's noindex is independent of the layout-level meta — it lives at the route level. Test in `tests/api/canary-noindex.test.ts` (already in P5.4); re-run as part of D4 verification.

### Sunset checklist (DUE 2026-05-30)

- [ ] Delete `site/src/routes/leaderboard/+server.ts` (the 302 redirect)
- [ ] Delete the `LEGACY_LEADERBOARD_ROUTES` alias from `site/src/lib/server/sse-routes.ts` (architect I1 — sunset alongside the redirect)
- [ ] Verify `site/tests/build/redirect-sunset.test.ts` passes (it asserts the redirect file is gone within the 14-day reminder window before 2026-05-30)
- [ ] Update `site/CHANGELOG.md` with a sunset entry
  • [ ] Step 3: Verify mkdocs build
  • [ ] Step 4: Commit
git -C /u/Git/CentralGauge add docs/site/operations.md
git -C /u/Git/CentralGauge commit -m "docs(site/operations): post-cutover verification + SSE/cache transient notes (P5.5)"

Append a new "P5.5 implementation notes" section:

## P5.5 implementation notes

The cutover ran 2026-04-30. Reference plan: `docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-30-p5-5-cutover.md`.

### Lessons

- {fill in after the deploy lands}

### Sunset checklist (post-cutover)

- The `/leaderboard` 302 redirect (`site/src/routes/leaderboard/+server.ts`) MUST be deleted by **2026-05-30**. The CI guard `site/tests/build/redirect-sunset.test.ts` enforces this — fails 14 days BEFORE sunset (2026-05-16) to force operator attention.
- ALSO delete the `LEGACY_LEADERBOARD_ROUTES` alias from `site/src/lib/server/sse-routes.ts` (architect I1 — its only purpose was the SSE stale-tab support during the sunset window).
- When deleting, also remove:
  - `tests/api/leaderboard-redirect.test.ts` (becomes meaningless)
  - The `redirect-sunset.test.ts` file itself once it's served its purpose

### Sitemap maintenance

The sitemap (`.svelte-kit/cloudflare/sitemap.xml`) is generated at build time by `npx tsx scripts/build-sitemap.ts` and is **not committed** (architect I9). To add a new public route:

1. Edit `site/scripts/build-sitemap.ts` — add the path to `SITEMAP_ROUTES` (alphabetized).
2. Run `npm run build` locally — verify `.svelte-kit/cloudflare/sitemap.xml` contains the new route.
3. Run `npx vitest run --config vitest.build.config.ts scripts/build-sitemap.test.ts` — the deterministic snapshot test catches schema drift.
4. Commit ONLY the script change (the artifact is gitignored).

CI catches drift through the unit test — there is no separate `git diff` guard.

### Structured data evolution

The layout-level WebSite + Organization JSON-LD (`StructuredData.svelte`) covers every page. Per-page schemas (Article / Dataset / SoftwareApplication for /runs/:id, /models/:slug, /tasks/:id) are deferred to P6.

### Visual-regression baselines regenerated post-cutover

`tests/e2e/__screenshots__/visual-regression.spec.ts/home-*.png` (4 PNGs) were regenerated in commit `<sha>` via `playwright test --update-snapshots`. The pre-cutover screenshot directory contained only `.gitkeep` (no committed baselines). The post-cutover DOM differs from any conceivable `/leaderboard` baseline due to (a) JSON-LD `<script>` tags added in C2 and (b) `<link rel="canonical">` added in C3, so a `git mv` rename would not have worked even if pre-cutover PNGs had existed.
